papers.title_en: SSD: Spatially Speculative Decoding Accelerates Autoregressive Image Generation
papers.authors: Shilong Xiang, Zirui Zhang, Lijun Yu, Chengzhi Mao
papers.evidence: 对视觉语言模型中自回归图像生成应用投机解码
papers.tldr: 自回归图像生成模型将图像展平为1D序列，丢失了固有的2D空间局部性，导致推理时计算瓶颈严重。本文提出空间推测解码（SSD），通过同时预测相邻水平与下方token，利用2D空间相关性，突破了内存墙限制。在DPG-Bench和GenEval上，该方法实现了高达13.3倍的加速，同时保持高生成保真度。这项工作表明，尊重视觉的几何结构能大幅提升计算效率，为实时高分辨率自回归生成模型奠定了基础。
papers.abstract_en: Autoregressive models excel in visual generation by treating images as 1D sequences of discrete tokens, mirroring language modeling. However, this flattening discards the intrinsic 2D spatial locality of visual signals, creating severe computational bottlenecks during inference. We introduce Spatially Speculative Decoding (SSD), a framework that aligns the predictive objective with the natural geometry of images. Rather than predicting only the immediate next token in a 1D sequence, our model simultaneously predicts the adjacent horizontal token and the token directly below it. By capitalizing on this 2D spatial correlation, spatially speculative decoding overcomes the memory wall in visual inference. Our approach accelerates autoregressive image generation by up to 13.3x while maintaining high fidelity on DPG-Bench and GenEval. Our results suggest that respecting the underlying geometry of vision unlocks massive computational efficiencies, paving the way for real-time, high-resolution autoregressive generative models.

papers.title_en: Forget Without Compromise: Nexus Sampling for Streaming KV-Cache Eviction Under Fixed Budgets
papers.authors: Duc Duong, Hoang Anh Duy Le, Jianwen Xie, Anshumali Shrivastava, Zhaozhuo Xu
papers.evidence: 针对长上下文大语言模型推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
papers.tldr: 长上下文LLM的KV缓存受固定内存限制，现有确定性top-K驱逐方法会永久移除重要token。本文提出Nexus Sampling，通过Nexus评分迭代发现桥接token，并采用加权蓄水库采样以概率保留token。在80%驱逐率下，LongBench上性能与密集注意力差距在1%以内，检索任务上超越基线，缓存内存减少10倍。该方法无需训练，在保持性能的同时显著降低缓存需求，适用于流式推理。
papers.abstract_en: Long-context and agentic LLM workloads push the KV cache past any fixed memory budget, forcing the inference stack to permanently evict tokens at every step of a continuous-inference stream. Existing methods all share the same template, a per-step direct-attention score followed by deterministic top-$K$ selection, which converts a single below-cutoff step into an irreversible verdict and permanently erases any subtly important token that direct attention cannot single out from noise. To address this challenge, we propose Nexus Sampling, a training-free eviction method that pairs Nexus scoring, an iterative walk over direct attention that surfaces bridge tokens, with weighted reservoir sampling, which retains tokens with inclusion probability in place of deterministic top-$K$. Theoretically, we show that Nexus Sampling dominates deterministic top-$K$ in long-run survival of subtly important tokens. Empirically, at 80% KV cache eviction, Nexus Sampling matches dense attention within 1% on LongBench while outperforming top-$K$ baselines on retrieval-heavy tasks, with up to 10x smaller per-sequence cache memory.

papers.title_en: Spectral Evolution-Guided Token Pruning in Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.authors: Bin Chen, Yuxiang Cai, Yadan Luo, Yi Zhang, Jianwei Yin, Zhi Chen
papers.evidence: 通过token剪枝加速多模态大模型推理
papers.tldr: 多模态大语言模型面临视觉标记冗余问题，现有单层信号剪枝方法忽略跨层变换且存在位置偏差。本文提出基于跨层谱演化（CLSE）的无训练剪枝框架，在频域量化标记表征跨层动态，保留语义活跃标记。在图像和视频基准上，CLSE在激进标记减少下保持或提升性能，同时降低FLOPs、KV缓存和延迟，实现效率与精度的优越权衡。
papers.abstract_en: Reducing visual token redundancy is critical for accelerating Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) without degrading cross-modal reasoning performance. Existing token pruning methods typically rely on single-layer signals, such as attention scores or token similarities, which overlook the cross-layer transformation of visual representations and may exhibit positional bias in multimodal token sequences. To address this limitation, we propose a training-free token pruning framework based on Cross-Layer Spectral Evolution (CLSE). Instead of measuring token importance from single-layer feature magnitudes, CLSE quantifies how token representations evolve across Transformer layers in the frequency domain. This evolution reflects the transition from high-frequency structural details to low-frequency semantic abstractions. We observe that tokens with stronger spectral redistribution across layers are more likely to be semantically active and should therefore be preserved. By modeling cross-layer token dynamics, CLSE provides a stable importance criterion that mitigates positional bias. Extensive experiments on both image and video benchmarks demonstrate that CLSE achieves a superior trade-off between efficiency and accuracy under aggressive token reduction. Across multiple MLLMs, CLSE reduces FLOPs, KV cache memory, and latency while maintaining competitive or improved performance.
